template_rationale: Android is the longest (346 lines) and covers the broadest section taxonomy; used as structural template, with the explicit caveat that some sections (DB encryption, ProGuard) are Android-only and remain n_a on other platforms.

sections.section: Page intro / what this gives you

ios.gap_description: Manual install via xcframework documented as alternative.

flutter.gap_description: No alternative install path documented. Either add (if one exists, e.g. git ref in pubspec) or explicitly state pubspec is the only supported method.

web.gap_description: All three package managers (npm/yarn/pnpm) covered in primary install section via tabs.

ios.gap_description: Bridging header mentioned only inside the Obj-C section; iOS-specific concerns (Info.plist permissions, Podfile if not SPM) not consolidated.

flutter.gap_description: Flutter wraps native iOS+Android — quickstart should mention Podfile platform version, Android minSdk bump, and any MainActivity changes if required.

web.gap_description: No bundler / CSP / TypeScript config notes. Best-Practices section partially covers this but as principles, not concrete configuration.

ios.gap_description: Page links out to /authentication for SDK init. A quickstart should include the smallest runnable init snippet inline.

android.gap_description: AmityCoreClient.setup() is shown inside Database Encryption examples but not in a standalone 'first call' section.

flutter.gap_description: No SDK init code shown at all.

web.gap_description: No Client.createClient() example shown; page jumps from install to best-practices.

sections.structural_note: MISSING ON ALL FOUR. This is a structural gap, not a per-platform one. Recommend a single restructure task that adds a standardised 'Initialize the SDK' section to all four pages.

ios.gap_description: Verified against AmitySDKIOS source — no public DB encryption API. Confirmed by absence of DBEncryption / dbEncryption symbols in Swift sources.

flutter.gap_description: Verified against Amity-Social-Cloud-SDK-Flutter-Internal — no encryption symbols in Dart sources.

web.gap_description: Browser storage encryption is out of scope; no equivalent API in TypeScript SDK.

android.gap_description: ProGuard covered. Missing Kotlin coroutines vs RxJava guidance for newer projects.

flutter.gap_description: No platform-channel / native-init notes. Flutter SDKs typically have iOS Podfile minimums and Android Gradle nuances worth a sentence.

web.gap_description: Framework Compatibility cards cover this; could be more concrete with per-framework init snippets.

ios.gap_description: No standalone best-practices section. Consider whether this is needed at the quickstart level or belongs in a separate guide.

android.gap_description: Same as iOS — not present at quickstart level.

sections.structural_note: Only web has this. IA decision: either add to all four (consistency) or move web's into a separate guide (consistency in the other direction). Tier C — needs human PM decision.
All four use identical 4-card CardGroup (Authentication / Chat / Social / Video). Good consistency baseline.

ios.gap_description: Has 2 accordions (Build, Runtime). Missing the Installation-issues accordion that the other three have.

android.gap_description: 3 accordions (Build, Runtime, RxJava). RxJava is platform-specific so reasonable.

sections.structural_note: Accordion naming is inconsistent ('Build' vs 'Installation'; 'Platform' on Flutter only). Tier A normalization task: harmonize the common-case accordions to a shared set, allow platform-specific extras.

key_findings: STRUCTURAL: All four quickstarts lack a concrete 'Initialize the SDK' section. They all link out to /authentication instead. This is the single biggest content gap — a quickstart that doesn't show the first call isn't a quickstart.
STRUCTURAL: Best-practices content exists only on web. Either harmonize up (add to all) or harmonize down (move web's to a separate guide).
ACCURATE-BY-OMISSION: DB encryption is Android-only at the SDK level. Verified against source. The absence on iOS/Flutter/Web is NOT a doc gap — it would be a doc fabrication to add it.

FLUTTER: Flutter quickstart is dramatically under-built (80 lines vs Android's 346). It's missing project configuration, SDK init, platform-specific notes, and alternative install. This page should be prioritised regardless of tier.
MECHANICAL WINS: Troubleshooting accordion naming inconsistency and Flutter's missing 'alternative install' clarification are both fully Tier A — they don't require SDK-grounding.
